scope: Press first-party source and development tooling

excludedRules.evidence: 1497 findings are dominated by dynamic service, options, browser callback, and test-harness signatures; enabling it would require a semantic cleanup campaign rather than a correctness gate.
861 findings primarily represent intentional best-effort browser cleanup and storage fallbacks whose failure must not replace the primary operation.
68 findings occur in regular-expression and fixture literals where mechanical edits create churn without strengthening runtime correctness.
8 findings are deliberate regular-expression literals; the repository contract tests cover the accepted inputs more directly.
3 findings are deliberate parser and sanitization character ranges covered by focused behavior tests.
57 findings arise in stateful parser, DOM, and compatibility flows where a repository-wide rewrite is not an incremental lint boundary.
4 findings are compatibility error translations; the public error contract is tested and does not expose Error.cause.

noGrowth.policy: All enabled rules have a zero-violation baseline. Every excluded-rule diagnostic is bound to its rule, repository path, semantic lexical owner, stable local structural context, diagnostic message, rule-specific semantic anchor, and duplicate occurrence. The checked-in head inventory must match exactly; merge-base comparison rejects new debt, cross-callback moves, changed dead-assignment sources, and equal-count remove-and-add swaps while allowing unrelated sibling insertion, formatting-only changes, and intentional debt reduction.

inlineConfiguration.policy: Source comments cannot disable project lint rules. The quality-only real-config probe proves a used eslint-disable-next-line cannot hide no-undef while a clean sample remains at zero errors and warnings.

eslint.removalCondition: Re-evaluate an excluded rule only when its evidence no longer applies; do not replace repository contract tests with generic lint unless the same invariant remains covered.

noGrowth.policy: New candidate files must pass Prettier. After bootstrap, an established path baseline may retain only untouched files or transfer through an exact-content rename; touching a legacy file requires formatting it and removing it from the baseline.

prettier.removalCondition: Remove a path after formatting that file intentionally; avoid release-bearing mass reformat commits.

composerIdentityOwnership.policy: Every Composer identity scenario, setup block, helper assertion, literal product-source dependency, and manifest owner is bound to an exact reviewed identity. Domain owners are ordinary non-symlink files with bounded lines and source fan-in; dynamic loaders, direct filesystem reads, owner-to-owner imports, unlisted files, cap loosening, and equal-count inventory swaps fail closed.

evidence.command: node scripts/probe-typescript-debt.mjs using allowJs/checkJs/noEmit/skipLibCheck, ES2023, Preserve modules, Bundler resolution, and ES2023/DOM/DOM.Iterable libs
evidence.reason: Repository-wide checkJs would create a second large suppression baseline across dynamic browser boundaries; even after separating transitive vendor output, first-party code retains 903 diagnostics in 82 files.

noGrowth.policy: The checked-in baseline must exactly match the current deterministic root graph and diagnostics. CI rejects every new path/code/message key and any count growth relative to the merge base; intentional reductions are persisted only through the reviewed --write-baseline workflow.

suppressions.policy: Every tracked probe root and every repository-local assets/js source loaded into the TypeScript program is parsed with TypeScript semantics. Real suppression comments fail closed; identical text in strings or ordinary prose is not a directive.

revisitWhen: Composer and editor modules have been grouped behind stable directory and service boundaries
Each extracted module can adopt checkJs or ts-check without a repository-wide compatibility baseline
